text: Honey, you'll never believe what I got.
Honey, the vacuum's broken again.
How did you know I wanted one of these?
Donna, I've seen you read about it in Wired Magazine.
You've been talking nonstop about how it knows what to avoid,
that it knows the difference between a shoe and between a tool,
that it knows...
Shut up! Open the box, I want to see!
We started Matic because we kept seeing the same thing over and over again.
Those so-called smart rackumes?
Well, they're not actually that smart.
They bump through our homes, turning everyday objects into insurmountable obstacles.
Shoes, rugs, toys, they can't even handle the basics.
And boy, do they love choking on wires.
So we asked ourselves a very simple question.
What would it take to build a robot that is actually intelligent,
that we don't need to babysit, that doesn't require any pre-cleaning,
that we can actually trust to clean our homes without making a bigger mess?
A robot that simply just works.
We realized that if a robot is going to work in human homes,
it has to see the world the way humans do.
That's why we give Matic real vision.
It has five onboard cameras so it can see and adapt in real time,
adjust to the chaos that comes with pets and kids.
We couldn't understand why we have to jeopardize our family's privacy
just to keep our floors clean.
So we made sure Matic processes all the visual data on the device itself.
So everything it sees and learns stays within the four walls of our homes.
We aren't just building cool demos of some big futuristic dream.
We are shipping and we're doubling down on what already makes life better for busy families.
We're building the robot that we always wish existed for us.
One that gives you your time back, allows you to relax,
makes living in a home with clean floors effortless.
And the best part?
Matic is already working in more than 6,000 homes,
cleaning more than 100 million square feet.
More than 6,000 families trust and use Matic.
And that's why this round happened.
